steps.action: Grep server.go for statusCanceled constant definition
steps.observed: const statusCanceled = "canceled" at line 28 in internal/server/server.go

steps.action: Grep openclaw-manifest.json for status enum values
steps.observed: Line 355: "canceled" in the job status enum array. No instances of "cancelled" in status value fields. ("cancelled" appears only in event type name "job.cancelled" and description text, which is acceptable per spec.)

steps.action: Verify manifest has no "cancelled" as a status value
steps.observed: All status values use "canceled" (single L): line 355 (enum), line 500 (cancel_job response), line 674 (WebSocket event status). The word "cancelled" only appears in event type names ("job.cancelled") and description text.
Line 571: respondWithSuccess(w, "cancelJob", map[string]any{"id": jobID, "status": statusCanceled}) where statusCanceled = "canceled"
Test PASS (0.00s). Test at line 624 of server_coverage_test.go verifies: if canceled.Status != statusCanceled { t.Errorf(...) }
Line 237: job.Status = statusCanceled. Line 233 checks terminal states including statusCanceled.

steps.action: Check server.go cancel handler WebSocket broadcast
steps.expected: broadcastEvent uses statusCanceled for status field
steps.observed: Lines 563-569: broadcastEvent(s.wsHub, map[string]any{"type": "job.cancelled", "jobId": jobID, "status": statusCanceled, ...}) where statusCanceled = "canceled"
Lines 635-641: broadcastEvent with "status": statusCanceled when job is cancelled during progress updates
Lines 672-677: broadcastEvent with "status": statusCanceled in the error cancellation path

steps.expected: Manifest shows status value "canceled" for job.cancelled event
steps.observed: Line 674 in manifest: "value": "canceled" for the status field of job.cancelled event. Event type name "job.cancelled" (double L) is acceptable — it's the event name, not the status value.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all 3 passed. The manifest status enum uses "canceled" (single L) matching the code constant statusCanceled = "canceled". API responses use the statusCanceled constant for all cancel-related operations. WebSocket events broadcast status: "canceled" (single L) while the event type name remains "job.cancelled" (double L) as expected per spec.
